# v2.3.0 — Plan of Work

**Source:** Deep analysis of v2.2.0 run log and verification against current repository state
**Analyzed by:** GitHub Copilot (Claude Opus 4.6)
**Date:** 2026-04-06

---

## Context

The v2.2.0 run produced 10 gap findings and 8 workflow recommendations (R1–R8) plus a 10-item codebase refactoring plan (R1–R10).

**Codebase refactoring (R1–R10):** Fully implemented across v2.2.0/v2.2.1/v2.2.2 refactoring rounds. Package structure, naming, model consolidation, defensive copies, formatting, health endpoint — all done and verified.

**Workflow recommendations (R1–R8):** 4 of 8 remain unimplemented — and they are the 4 highest-impact items. This plan targets them.

---

## Priority Actions

### P1 — High Impact (Do First)

#### P1-A: Split Coder Responsibilities

**Problem:** Coder agent has 12+ responsibilities per invocation. This is the #1 system bottleneck across 4 consecutive versions. The model (GPT-5.3-Codex) degrades under prompt complexity — building mega-commands, stalling, failing to return handoffs.

**Goal:** Reduce Coder to 4 core responsibilities: (1) implement code, (2) write tests, (3) run verification scripts, (4) return changed files + status.

**File:** `.github/agents/java-coder.agent.md`

**Remove from Coder:**
- Starting the application
- Running curl / smoke checks
- Updating `scripts/smoke-test.sh`
- Writing structured verification logs with parsed output
- Updating implementation report coder-owned sections
- Running `scripts/final-check.sh` (move to TL pre-Reviewer gate)

**Simplify Verification section to:**
- Run `scripts/verify-quick.sh` after each slice
- Run `scripts/record-verification.sh` (see P1-C) to log evidence
- Report: `PASS` or `FAIL (last 10 lines of output)`
- Do not parse Maven output with bash

**Simplify Handoff to:**
```
(1) Feature name
(2) Status: complete / blocked
(3) Changed files (list)
(4) Deviations (none, or one-line each)
(5) Blockers (none, or description)
```

**File:** `.github/agents/team-lead.agent.md`

**Add to TL after Coder handoff:**
- TL runs `scripts/final-check.sh`
- TL starts the app and runs `scripts/smoke-test.sh`
- TL writes/updates the verification log
- TL updates the implementation report
- This consolidates evidence-gathering into the orchestrator that already does spot-checks

---

#### P1-B: Add Code Quality Review to Reviewer

**Problem:** Reviewer Phase 2 focuses entirely on execution-based validation (run scripts, check pass/fail). It performs no meaningful code review. In v2.2.0, the Reviewer missed every code quality issue the user caught manually (naming chaos, duplicate catch blocks, model proliferation, unnecessary layers).

**Goal:** Make Reviewer a real code reviewer, not an expensive terminal runner.

**File:** `.github/agents/reviewer.agent.md`

**Add a "Code Quality Review" subsection to Phase 2, after execution-based checks:**

```markdown
### Code Quality Review (Phase 2 only)

After execution-based checks pass, review the changed source files for:
- Naming consistency against `documentation/code-guidance.md` naming conventions
- Model count — flag when a new model is a 1:1 mirror of an existing one
- Duplicate code patterns — identical catch blocks, repeated null-check chains, copy-pasted logic
- Over-engineering — unnecessary wrappers, abstractions, or layers for single-use operations
- Package placement — files in correct packages per `code-guidance.md` package structure
- Missing or misleading log context

Every finding must reference the specific file and line. Mark `FAIL` if any finding has material impact on maintainability.
```

---

#### P1-C: Add `scripts/record-verification.sh` Helper

**Problem:** Coder builds 15-line bash scripts to capture output, parse exit codes, and write structured verification log entries. These scripts fail repeatedly and waste 4%+ of context on retries. This is the "mega-command" pattern identified in v2.2.0 Gap 5.

**Goal:** Provide a reusable script so the Coder never needs to build custom bash pipelines.

**File:** `scripts/record-verification.sh` (new)

**Script behavior:**
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Usage: scripts/record-verification.sh <slice-name> <feature-name> <command...>
# Runs the command, captures exit code, appends structured entry to verification log.
set -euo pipefail

SLICE_NAME="$1"; FEATURE_NAME="$2"; shift 2
LOG="artifacts/implementation-reports/${FEATURE_NAME}-verification.log"
mkdir -p "$(dirname "$LOG")"

echo "=== Slice: ${SLICE_NAME} | $(date -u) ===" >> "$LOG"
echo "command: $*" >> "$LOG"

set +e
"$@" 2>&1 | tail -n 20
EXIT_CODE=${PIPESTATUS[0]}
set -e

echo "exit_code: ${EXIT_CODE}" >> "$LOG"
echo "" >> "$LOG"
exit "${EXIT_CODE}"
```

**Update Coder instruction to reference it:**
_"Run `scripts/record-verification.sh <slice> <feature> scripts/verify-quick.sh` instead of building custom bash pipelines."_

---

### P2 — Medium Impact

#### P2-A: Eliminate Triple Verification

**Problem:** Three agents run overlapping verification: Coder (`verify-quick.sh` + `final-check.sh`), TL (`mvn test`), Reviewer (`verify-quick.sh` + `final-check.sh` + startup + smoke). This burns ~6-8% of context per feature on duplicated work.

**Goal:** Each verification command runs exactly once, owned by one agent.

**Ownership model:**

| Check | Owner | When |
|---|---|---|
| `scripts/verify-quick.sh` | Coder | After each slice |
| `scripts/final-check.sh` | TL | After Coder handoff, before Reviewer |
| App startup + `scripts/smoke-test.sh` | TL | After final-check passes |
| Code quality review (reading code) | Reviewer | Phase 2 |
| `scripts/final-check.sh` (re-run) | Reviewer | Phase 2, only if TL evidence is suspect |

**Files to change:**
- `.github/agents/team-lead.agent.md` — replace "independently rerun `mvn test`" with "run `scripts/final-check.sh` once"
- `.github/agents/reviewer.agent.md` — replace mandatory script re-execution with "validate TL evidence, re-run only if suspect"
- `.github/instructions/local-quality-rules.instructions.md` — update expected agent workflow section to match new ownership

---

#### P2-B: Simplify Coder Evidence Instruction

**Problem:** The instruction _"record ONLY: the command, exit code, test count, and failure summary (max 5 lines per command)"_ causes GPT-5.3-Codex to interpret this as "I need to parse Maven output into structured data with bash." The 5-line limit + structured output requirement is the direct trigger for mega-commands.

**Goal:** Replace with a simple instruction that removes the incentive to build parsers.

**File:** `.github/agents/java-coder.agent.md`

**Replace the Evidence Gathering constraint with:**

```markdown
- **Evidence Gathering**: After running a script, report: `PASS` or `FAIL`. If `FAIL`, paste the last 10 lines of terminal output. Do not build custom bash pipelines to parse or format command output. Use `scripts/record-verification.sh` when recording to the verification log.
```

**Remove the separate verification log section header instruction** (the `record-verification.sh` script handles this).

---

#### P2-C: Fill v2.3.0 Addressed Findings Table

**Problem:** The `Addressed Findings From Previous Log Analysis` table in `.github/agentic-flow/logs/v2.3.0.md` is empty. Future comparisons lose traceability.

**File:** `.github/agentic-flow/logs/v2.3.0.md`

**Fill the table with:**

| Issue spotted | How was addressed | What pain point should be solved |
|---|---|---|
| Gap 10 — No formatting tool | Added Spotless with palantir-java-format, `scripts/format-code.sh`, `scripts/final-check.sh` | Eliminates indentation/formatting issues permanently |
| Gap 6 — No package structure reference | Added Package Structure And Model Gate to `code-guidance.md` | Architect and Coder follow documented structure, not ad-hoc |
| Gap 8 — Command naming pattern | Added `*Input` suffix convention to code-guidance, renamed `CreateIssueCommand` → `CreateIssueInput` | No misleading CQRS implications |
| Gap 3 — Reviewer adds no value (partial) | Added `flow-reviewer.agent.md` for flow-level analysis | Dedicated review capability for system improvements |
| Run observability gap | Created `log-template.md` as reusable baseline for run logs | Structured, comparable run analysis across versions |

---

### P3 — Lower Impact / Evaluate

#### P3-A: Evaluate Coder Model Change

**Problem:** GPT-5.3-Codex degrades under the current prompt complexity. Two consecutive runs of terminal command degradation and stalled handoffs.

**Approach:**
1. Implement P1-A (simplify prompt) first
2. Run one feature with simplified prompt on GPT-5.3-Codex
3. If degradation persists, try Claude Sonnet 4.6 (same as Architect) — better at following complex multi-step instructions
4. Compare: run time, context used, coder retry loops, terminal failures

**File:** `.github/agents/java-coder.agent.md` (model line only)

---

#### P3-B: Pin Reviewer to Stable Model

**Problem:** `Gemini 3.1 Pro (Preview)` is a preview model. Preview models can change behavior between runs. For a critical quality gate, this introduces non-determinism.

**Action:** When Gemini 3.1 Pro reaches GA, switch to the stable release. If GA is not available, consider Claude Sonnet 4.6 or GPT-5.4 as alternatives.

**File:** `.github/agents/reviewer.agent.md` (model line only)

---

## Execution Order

| Phase | Actions | Risk | Prerequisite |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | P1-C: Create `record-verification.sh` | None (new script) | — |
| 2 | P1-A: Simplify Coder prompt + move duties to TL | Medium (workflow change) | P1-C done |
| 3 | P2-B: Simplify Coder evidence instruction | Low (prompt wording) | P1-C done |
| 4 | P2-A: Eliminate triple verification | Medium (ownership change) | P1-A done |
| 5 | P1-B: Add code quality review to Reviewer | Low (additive) | P2-A done (so Reviewer scope is clear) |
| 6 | P2-C: Fill addressed findings table | None (documentation) | Anytime |
| 7 | P3-A: Evaluate Coder model | Depends on run | P1-A + P2-B done |
| 8 | P3-B: Pin Reviewer model | Low | Next feature run |

| Metric | v2.2.0 result | v2.3.0 target |
|---|---|---|
| Total run time | 1h 40m | < 1h |
| Context used | ~40% | < 25% |
| Coder retry loops | 3+ (degraded) | 0–1 |
| Coder terminal mega-commands | YES (recurring) | NONE |
| Reviewer code quality findings | 0 (user caught all) | ≥ 3 per feature |
| Agents running same verification command | 3 | 1 per command |
| Manual code fixes after run | 10+ issues | < 3 |
